reply_text: You people always use that same excuse. We’re allowed to want something that we love to get the proper treatment it deserves. If a manga that’s known for its insanely creative and well choreographed fights gets lackluster production quality, it’s justified to criticize it. If I go to a restaurant that’s known for high quality food and I instead get McDonald’s quality, it’s fine to be unsatisfied. We got shows like Blue Box and My Dress Up Darling that are literally ROMANCE anime, yet have insanely smooth animation that’s better than Sakamoto Days when that’s not even supposed to be the main focal point. JJK is partly so massive BECAUSE of its great animation; You wanna know why that works? Because action was always the main selling point of the manga, and the anime elevated the source material. Solo Leveling, DanDaDan, Frieren, and JJK are all shows that are heavily praised and successful because of their great animation. I’m not even blaming the studio even though I personally don’t think TMS is fit to handle such a big and beloved project. I’m blaming Japan’s terrible work culture, and how animators often get treated terrible and work under horrid conditions with limited time to actually make a great product.
